description_long: This is a large, rectangular stone block with a hollowed-out interior, resembling a trough or planter. The exterior surface is rough and speckled, typical of granite or similar stone, with a consistent light gray coloration. The block has thick, straight walls and a flat base, with a small notch visible on one of the shorter sides, possibly for drainage or lifting. The interior cavity is deep and rectangular, with the same stone texture as the exterior. The overall construction appears solid and heavy, suitable for use as a barrier or planter in urban environments. There are no visible engravings, markings, or significant weathering on the surface.
description: A large, hollow rectangular stone block with a rough, speckled gray surface and a deep interior cavity. It features thick walls and a small notch on one short side.
description_view_0: Front view showing the long, flat face of the stone block and the depth of the hollowed interior.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the stone walls and the rectangular shape of the cavity.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the hollow interior visible and the notch less prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the notch on the short wall and the depth of the interior cavity.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or public park planter area
attribute_installation_method: set in place on a leveled concrete or compacted gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and uniformly textured.
